extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial sharp drop, the series stays low (below about 0.25) for a relatively long mid section (roughly indices 15–40) before rising again.  
R2: In the last third of the series (roughly indices 60–100), values remain moderate (around 0.35–0.55) without returning to the initial high peak level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial sharp drop, the series does not stay low for long: it rises back to mid-level values (around 0.35–0.55) earlier (roughly before index 30).  
R2: In the last third of the series, values remain in a narrow mid band (around 0.35–0.5) and lack the later pronounced upward swing seen in Class 0.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial sharp drop (first ~10–15% of the series), the signal reaches its minimum around the first third of the series and then rises to a mid‑level “plateau” (about half of the initial peak) for the middle part of the series (roughly between 40–80% of the length).  
R2: In the final 15–20% of the series, the signal does not fall below this mid‑level plateau and typically stays flat or increases slightly (no clear downward trend at the end).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial sharp drop (first ~10–15% of the series), the signal reaches its minimum around the first third of the series and then rises to a mid‑level “plateau” (about half of the initial peak) that is clearly visible by the middle of the series (around 40–60% of the length).  
R2: In the final 15–20% of the series, the signal shows a slight but clear downward drift from that mid‑level plateau (the end values are typically lower than the local maxima around the series’ midpoint).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the early sharp drop and valley (around index 30–40), the series rises and then stays roughly flat or gently oscillating, without a strong monotonic increase, staying mostly below about 0.55 for the remaining time.
R2: There is no pronounced, sustained peak near the very end; the last 10–15% of the series does not show a clear upward trend forming a new high relative to the mid‑segment plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the early sharp drop and valley (around index 30–40), the series rises and then forms a mid‑segment hump/plateau, then gradually declines or levels off toward the end, with no late rise above this mid‑segment hump.
R2: Any local maximum in the last 10–15% of the series is lower than the main mid‑segment peak and does not continue into an upward trend.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the early sharp drop (around index 5–15), the series bottom stays low (near its minimum) for a relatively long segment and then rises smoothly into mid-series.  
R2: In the last third of the series, values are roughly flat or gently rising, without a pronounced hump that clearly exceeds the mid-series level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the early sharp drop (around index 5–15), the series bottom is brief; it quickly rebounds into a clear mid-series hump/plateau that rises well above the preceding low.  
R2: In the last third of the series, values form a visible hump or plateau that is clearly higher than the preceding trough but not strongly increasing toward the very end (more “bump and level” than “steady climb”).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial sharp drop and partial recovery, the series stays roughly flat or only mildly increasing in the last third (no clear sustained upward trend near the end).
R2: The minimum point occurs roughly in the middle of the series and is followed by a smooth, gradual rise without a steep single jump.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial sharp drop and partial recovery, the series shows a clear sustained upward trend in the middle part, reaching a plateau higher than the preceding flat segment.
R2: There is at least one noticeable steep upward jump from near the minimum (in the first half of the series) to the middle plateau, sharper than the gradual rises seen in Class 0.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial drop and rise, the values in the middle-to-late part of the series (roughly indices 50–80) remain relatively flat or slightly decreasing, staying around 0.4–0.5 without a clear upward trend.  
R2: Near the very end of the series (last ~10–15 points), there is **no** pronounced local maximum that clearly exceeds the preceding plateau (no strong final bump above about 0.5–0.55).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial drop and rise, the values in the middle portion (roughly indices 40–60) form a clear hump that peaks around 0.45–0.55 and then **decrease** toward later indices.  
R2: From that middle peak to the end of the series, the overall trend is gently **downward or flat**, without a late upward surge; end values are slightly lower than or similar to the mid-peak.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the early drop (around time 5–10), the series stays low and flat near its minimum for a relatively long middle segment (roughly from time 15 to about 35–40) before rising again.  
R2: In the late part (after about time 60), the series tends to stay roughly flat or only gently increasing, without a pronounced single peak higher than the mid‑segment rise.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the early drop (around time 5–10), the series reaches its minimum more briefly and begins rising again sooner, so the low “valley” segment (roughly time 15–35) is shorter than in Class 0.  
R2: In the late part (after about time 60), the series typically exhibits a clearer, more pronounced hump/peak in the mid‑to‑late region rather than remaining almost flat.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the early sharp drop (roughly time 0–20), the series reaches its minimum around time 30–40 and then rises to a plateau in the middle region (about time 50–80) whose values are typically between 0.35 and 0.55.  
R2: In the final segment (about time 80–100) the series either stays roughly flat or increases slightly, without any clear downward trend.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the early sharp drop (roughly time 0–20), the series reaches its minimum around time 30–40 and then rises to a middle plateau (about time 40–80) whose values are typically between 0.4 and 0.6.  
R2: In the final segment (about time 80–100) the series tends to be flat or slightly decreasing, with no consistent upward drift.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial sharp drop (around index 10–15), the series stays in a low “valley” region (values roughly between 0.1 and 0.25) for a relatively long segment (about indices 15–40).  
R2: In the last third of the series (after about index 60), values are roughly stable or gently increasing around a mid‑level band (about 0.4–0.55) without a strong downward tail.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial sharp drop, the low “valley” region (values roughly between 0.1 and 0.25) is shorter and ends earlier (before about index 35), followed by a faster rise.  
R2: In the last third of the series (after about index 60), values tend to gently decrease or remain flat around mid‑level (about 0.35–0.5), often forming a slight downward or flat tail rather than a clear upward trend.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial large drop, the series stays at a relatively low level (near its minimum) for a longer middle segment before rising again.
R2: In the final third of the series, values tend to rise and remain around or above the mid‑range (≈0.45–0.55) without a pronounced downward tail.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial large drop, the series reaches its minimum sooner and then starts a sustained rise earlier, without a long flat low plateau in the middle.
R2: In the final third of the series, values either slowly decrease or stay roughly flat around the mid‑range (≈0.40–0.50), often showing a slight downward bias rather than a late strong rise.
